behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watch and event emission for nested paths). A mocked watcher can emit subdirectory events even if real OS/watcher wiring would miss them, so Integration/Unit can falsely pass. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI/model judgment or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon process, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is local dedup policy for same-path requests within a time window; this is primarily map/time-window logic and can be validated at a single object/function boundary with a fake clock. Integration/System were considered but disqualified because the behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess, real model output, or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke dedup component twice with identical canonical file path and controlled timestamps (within window), assert first request accepted and second rejected; then advance fake clock past window and assert acceptance resumes.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-level duplicate event patterns. In-process fakes can manufacture idealized events and hide real coalescing/burst behavior, so lower levels miss true failures. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple FS events (e.g., write+rename/flush), capture processing side effects, and assert exactly one processing/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is real OS watcher lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/inode replacement/re-subscription). Mocks typically bypass watcher invalidation details and would hide failures. Higher levels are disqualified because no LLM/model decisions or agentic chaining are part of the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering guarantee depends on interaction between real arrival concurrency, filesystem timestamp semantics/resolution, and daemon queueing. A mocked source can hide race/order bugs present under real event timing. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI output dependence or multi-agent pipeline exists.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the watched tree, record observed processing order (e.g., via logs/output metadata), and assert it matches creation timestamp order (with deterministic tie-break rule if equal timestamps are expected).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at real filesystem/event timing boundary (partial writes, flush timing, possible temp-file/rename strategy). Stubs generally present complete content instantly and can mask premature reads. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior does not involve model judgment or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, open a file for incremental writes in the watched directory, trigger arrival early, keep writing chunks with delays, then close file; assert processing starts only after final close/stability condition and output reflects full final content.
